zh.Stanlow Abbey, Stanlow, Cheshire, Englan: 英國英格蘭爾斯坦洛福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Clare於1162年出生在英國肯特托布利其時，她的父親Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford46歲，母親Matilda de Saint-Hilaire30歲。她於1192年與Roger de Lacy結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1213年享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾斯坦洛福。

zh-hans.Stanlow Abbey, Stanlow, Cheshire, Englan: 英国英格兰爾斯坦洛福
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda de Clare于1162年出生在英国肯特托布利其时，她的父亲Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford46岁，母亲Matilda de Saint-Hilaire30岁。她于1192年与Roger de Lacy结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1213年享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾斯坦洛福。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Clare was born in 1162, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, her father, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, was 46 and her mother, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, was 30. She married Roger de Lacy in 1192. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1213, at the age of 51, and was buried in Stanlow, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Clare est née en 1162, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Roger de Lacy en 1192. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1213, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Stanlow, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda de Clare 1162 in Tonbridge, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford 46 und ihre Mutter Matilda de Saint-Hilaire 30. Sie hat 1192 Roger de Lacy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1213 im Alter von 51 Jahren und wurde in Stanlow, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Clare è nata il nel 1162 nel Tonbridge, Kent, England, sua padre, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Roger de Lacy nel 1192. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1213, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Stanlow, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de Clareが1162年にイギリスケントトンブリッジで生まれた時，父Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertfordは46歳，母Matilda de Saint-Hilaireは30歳であった。1192年にRoger de Lacyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1213年に51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアスタンロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Clare(이)가 1162년 영국 켄트 토브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Saint-Hilaire(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1192년 Roger de Lacy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1213년 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스탠로에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Clare nasceu em 1162, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger de Lacy em 1192. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1213, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Stanlow, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Clare родилась в 1162 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger de Lacy в 1192 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1213 г. в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности стэнлоу, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda de Clare nació en 1162, en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Roger de Lacy en 1192. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1213, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Stanlow, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
